Error corrected with
![Riverlane Logo](https://www.riverlane.com/assets/logos/logo_riverlane_white.svg)
We partner with the world's leading quantum computing manufacturers, qubit control specialists, national labs and HPC centres.
Together we are accelerating the industry to utility scale.
[Sign up for partnership and product updates](https://www.riverlane.com/error-corrected-with-riverlane#newsletterpartners)
[ ![](https://www.riverlane.com/error-corrected-with-riverlane) Blog Scaling Quantum Error Correction on Real Systems: Lessons from the UK Quantum Missions  ](https://www.riverlane.com/blog/scaling-quantum-error-correction-on-real-systems-lessons-from-the-uk-quantum-missions)
> “Scaling quantum computing systems towards utility-scale requires that QEC is effectively integrated with quantum computing control systems, and that's exactly what we've achieved with this project. Demonstrating that we can decode quantum errors in real-time on real quantum hardware with a high number of error detection rounds addresses a crucial bottleneck towards achieving fault-tolerant quantum computing.”
> David Rivas, CTO at Rigetti
## Partners across the quantum stack
* * *
Quantum computing manufacturers
![Alice & Bob](https://www.riverlane.com/media/w0jdbiuo/alice-bob-logo.svg?width=500&quality=80&v=1dc8971262b8b90)
![Diraq](https://www.riverlane.com/media/rsmozwul/diraq.png?width=500&quality=80&v=1dbc4be05ea40b0)
![Inflection](https://www.riverlane.com/media/atipkwxv/logo-inflection.svg?width=500&quality=80&v=1dbbd43712d8a30)
![IQM](https://www.riverlane.com/media/rfuib4nf/logo-iqm.svg?width=500&quality=80&v=1dbbd4372157930)
![OQC](https://www.riverlane.com/media/jsymjuzt/logo-oqc.svg?width=500&quality=80&v=1dbbd436f9bc6a0)
![Oxford Ionics \(transp\)](https://www.riverlane.com/media/xrhnrbm0/oxford-ionics.png?width=500&quality=80&v=1dbc4be05e1b530)
![Pasqal](https://www.riverlane.com/media/epqcbmuo/logo-soft-green.png?width=500&quality=80&v=1dbbd4371f06500)
![Rigetti](https://www.riverlane.com/media/lfodj0f3/logo-rigetti.svg?width=500&quality=80&v=1dbbd436fef3dd0)
HPC centres
![Berkeley Lab](https://www.riverlane.com/media/h4djajcw/berkeley_lab_logo.png?width=500&quality=80&v=1dce8b129eb8ab0)
![Fermilab](https://www.riverlane.com/media/iqgpobrp/fermilab_logo.png?width=500&quality=80&v=1dce8b1400f4430)
![MIT Lincoln](https://www.riverlane.com/media/j42puwzo/mit_lincoln_logo.png?width=500&quality=80&v=1dce8b156c5c7d0)
![OakRidge](https://www.riverlane.com/media/k4hlqnrg/logo-oak-ridge.svg?width=500&quality=80&v=1dbbd436f6af2a0)
Qubit control specialists
![QBlox](https://www.riverlane.com/media/4i0dmmlb/logo-qblox.svg?width=500&quality=80&v=1dbbd436fd32a50)
![Quantum Machines](https://www.riverlane.com/media/rf3k3atb/quantum-machines.svg?width=500&quality=80&v=1dca7f9c6f5e010)
![Zurich Instruments](https://www.riverlane.com/media/pflhbm0n/zurich-instruments.svg?width=500&quality=80&v=1dca7fa14feae90)
> “Real-time quantum error correction is the critical bridge between today's devices and tomorrow's utility-scale quantum computers. Through our collaboration with Riverlane on DECIDE, we've shown that integrating hardware-aware error detection with high-performance classical infrastructure can meaningfully improve system performance. This kind of tightly coupled innovation across the stack is exactly what's needed to unlock scalable quantum computing,”
> Simon Phillips, CTO at OQC
